sections.paragraph:
τυγχάνω [cp. τύχη]

—1. ‘be privileged to receive a benefit’, happen upon w. gen. Lk 20:35; Ac 24:2; 26:22; 27:3; 2 Ti 2:10; Hb 8:6; 11:35.

—2. ‘meet up with something in ordinary experience’, come upon w. neg. δυνάμεις οὐ τὰς τυχούσας unusual miracles, i.e. not the kind one encounters randomly Ac 19:11; sim. of hospitality: no ordinary kind 28:2.—The conditional phrase εἰ τύχοι in 1 Cor 14:10; 15:37 leaves the door open for assessment of the data and is appropriately rendered probably. In 16:6 the nt. ptc. τυχόν indicates contingency depending on what happens, if it works out.
